title: Lithocardium macrostachyum
taxonomy: Lithocardium macrostachyum is classified within the family Zygophyllaceae, which includes other plants commonly known as the catclaw or creosote bush family. The genus Lithocardium is a member of the subfamily Mimozygothys. The exact classification of Lithocardium macrostachyum may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a species within the Zygophyllaceae family. Further research is needed to confirm its exact classification within this family. The taxonomy of Lithocardium macrostachyum is still a subject of ongoing research and study.
uses: Lithocardium macrostachyum is traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Latin America. The plant is reported to have potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
